name: User Assumed a Privileged Access (AWS)
notes:
This rule triggers when a user with temporary privileged access is detected. On AWS, assume role returns temporary credentials to resources the user has requested access for. The user's temporary security credentials or access key will be added to the reference set "Temporary Access Keys" which by default has a time-to-live interval of 1 hour. Adjust the time depending on the environment's expiry configuration.

Note: The Low Privilege Role Names reference set must be populated with the relevant role names. Anything not defined in this Reference Set is considered as suspicious in terms of privileges.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Amazon AWS CloudTrail
when the event category for the event is one of the following Authentication.Role Assignment Success
when the event matches User Type (custom) is any of IAMUser
when the event matches Machine Identifier (custom) is N/A
when any of Role Name (custom) are contained in any of Low Privilege Role Names - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY User Assumed a Privileged Access (AWS) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Amazon AWS CloudTrail
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Authentication.Role Assignment Success
AND when the event matches User Type (custom) is any of IAMUser
AND when the event matches Machine Identifier (custom) is N/A
AND NOT when any of Role Name (custom) are contained in any of Low Privilege Role Names - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Add the "Target Access Key ID" of the event or flow payload to the Reference Set "Temporary Access Keys"
